reasoning: Less than a month before the election (scheduled for Nov 16, 2025), polling from major Chilean pollsters shows Johannes Kaiser consistently trailing significantly behind the frontrunners. According to a Cadem poll published on October 20, 2025, Jeannette Jara is leading with 26%, followed by José Antonio Kast with 22%, while Kaiser is in a distant tie for third place at 12%. Another late October poll by Pulso Ciudadano shows Kaiser with only 4.2% support. For Kaiser to win the first round, he would need to surpass both Jara and Kast, requiring an unprecedented and massive polling error or electoral shift within a three-week window. Given the stable lead of his opponents and his position far behind them in fourth place, the chance of him receiving the most votes in the first round is virtually zero. I am setting the probability at the minimum allowed bound of 2%.
